Patch Set 2:

I'm a little annoyed that this patch was touched without checking with me. It was a non-trivial amount of work to backport this big patch series to 2.12. It had been sitting ready to land for a while and now we've got a -1 on one of the first few patches in the series :-/

Patch Set 2:

> I'm a little annoyed that this patch was touched without checking
 > with me. It was a non-trivial amount of work to backport this big
 > patch series to 2.12. It had been sitting ready to land for a while
 > and now we've got a -1 on one of the first few patches in the
 > series :-/

Sorry, my mistake. I should have checked with you. I guess I didn't think through the consequences of grabbing this patch. Is there a way to mitigate the damage at this point?

Patch Set 2:

My preference would be to revert this patch back to patch set 1 so that all the test results/code review of the subsequent patches are valid again.

Then rebase LU-13002 and LU-12889 so that they are both on top of this patch.

Patch Set 2:

Do you know a clean way to do so? If I check out PS1 and try to push it back I'm getting "no new changes" error. I'm concerned if I amend commit message and push, all patches above this one in the series would need to be rebased and lose approval rating. I don't know another way though

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> I'm concerned if I amend commit message and push, all patches above this one in the
> series would need to be rebased and lose approval rating.

No, that does not happen.  If you push the exact v1 patch, at worst the v1 patch will be retested, it does not affect the later patches
Uploaded patch set 3.

No additional testing was requested

Patch Set 3:

> No Builds Executed

V1 of the patch was pushed by Serguei, with a minor "git commit --amend" to update the commit message to avoid the "no changes" issue.  This restored the Verified and Code-Review labels on the patch.  I killed the build in Jenkins within the 90s grace period to avoid it triggering a new build and test run, but could not do the same with Gerrit Janitor.

Patch Set 2: Verified-1

Failed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 7.9/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-72vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/8e84ae0d-00ce-4a93-855d-28cf4771ec7a ran 5 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-lnet.

Patch Set 3:

> > No Builds Executed
 > 
 > V1 of the patch was pushed by Serguei, with a minor "git commit
 > --amend" to update the commit message to avoid the "no changes"
 > issue.  This restored the Verified and Code-Review labels on the
 > patch.  I killed the build in Jenkins within the 90s grace period
 > to avoid it triggering a new build and test run, but could not do
 > the same with Gerrit Janitor.

Thanks Andreas. Unfortunately, a test failure on patch set 2 has now cleared the Verified flag.

Patch Set 3:

> Unfortunately, a test failure on patch set 2 has now cleared the Verified flag.

Sigh, the lack of a v3 build meant that the inflight v2 testing was not cancelled. It seems like a bug in Gerrit that a v2 failure can change the v3 status, unless that was because the status was inherited from v1 indirectly?

In any case, I will have to retrigger that v3 build+test now anyway, because just retesting the v2 session will not cause a +1 from Maloo on the v3 patch.

commit.subject: LU-12410 tests: Additional test cases for lnetctl and DLC
commit.message:
LU-12410 tests: Additional test cases for lnetctl and DLC

To faciliate the refactoring of a few lnetctl commands I wrote some
additional test cases for sanity-lnet.sh

Lustre-change: https://review.whamcloud.com/35386
Lustre-commit: 9cf6596da2b5d83db1ceea66c33cb8ab765a7f94

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-lnet
Signed-off-by: Chris Horn <hornc@cray.com>
Change-Id: I9f3f74420f89b824ee25b6547c0baa815ccfd948
